mechanism_of_action: Decreases hepatic glucose production by inhibiting gluconeogenesis, reduces intestinal absorption of glucose, and improves insulin sensitivity by increasing peripheral glucose uptake and utilization in muscle and adipose tissue.

pharmacokinetics.absorption: Oral bioavailability is 50-60 percent with absorption occurring primarily in the small intestine via saturable active transport; peak plasma levels occur in 2-3 hours.
pharmacokinetics.metabolism: Not metabolized by the liver or any cytochrome P450 enzymes; remains unchanged in the body.
pharmacokinetics.elimination: Excreted unchanged in urine via glomerular filtration and active tubular secretion with 90 percent renal clearance.
pharmacokinetics.half_life: 4.5 to 6.2 hours in patients with normal renal function; prolonged in renal impairment.

approved_indications: Type 2 diabetes mellitus as monotherapy or combination therapy
off_label_uses: Polycystic ovary syndrome to improve ovulation
Prevention of type 2 diabetes in high-risk individuals
clinical_use.standard_dosing: Initial 500 mg twice daily with meals, titrated by 500 mg weekly to 1000 mg twice daily; maximum 2550 mg per day in divided doses.

dose_adjustments.renal_impairment: Reduce dose if eGFR 30-45 mL/min/1.73 m2; contraindicated below 30 mL/min/1.73 m2
dose_adjustments.hepatic_impairment: Use with caution; avoid in severe liver disease due to lactic acidosis risk
dose_adjustments.elderly: Initiate at 500 mg daily and titrate slowly based on renal function

serious_adverse_effects: Lactic acidosis especially with renal impairment
Vitamin B12 deficiency with prolonged use
Hypoglycemia when combined with insulin or sulfonylureas

contraindications.condition: Severe renal impairment with eGFR less than 30 mL/min/1.73 m2
Acute or chronic metabolic acidosis including diabetic ketoacidosis
Known hypersensitivity to metformin or any component

black_box_warnings: Risk of lactic acidosis which can be fatal; assess renal function before initiation and periodically thereafter

drug_interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitor may reduce renal function leading to decreased metformin clearance and accumulation
drug_interactions.clinical_effect: Increased risk of lactic acidosis and hyperkalemia
drug_interactions.management: Monitor serum creatinine and eGFR at baseline and every 3 months; adjust metformin dose if eGFR declines
Withhold metformin for 48 hours after contrast procedure and resume only after renal function is confirmed stable

drug_interactions.mechanism: Alcohol inhibits gluconeogenesis and may potentiate metformin effects on lactate
drug_interactions.clinical_effect: Increased risk of hypoglycemia and lactic acidosis
drug_interactions.management: Limit alcohol intake to less than 2 drinks daily and monitor blood glucose

food_interactions.mechanism: May slightly delay absorption but does not reduce overall bioavailability
food_interactions.management: Administer with meals to minimize gastrointestinal adverse effects
Avoid excessive intake and counsel on moderation

environmental_considerations: Dispose via drug take-back programs to prevent water contamination
Metformin is not readily biodegradable in aquatic environments
Avoid flushing tablets to minimize ecological impact on waterways

what_to_do.action: Monitor renal function with eGFR calculation at baseline and every 3 to 6 months
what_to_do.rationale: Detects early decline allowing timely dose adjustment to prevent lactic acidosis
what_to_do.implementation: Order serum creatinine and calculate eGFR using CKD-EPI equation
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Reduced incidence of metformin-associated lactic acidosis
what_to_do.monitoring: Review results before each prescription refill

what_to_do.action: Assess vitamin B12 levels annually in patients on long-term therapy
what_to_do.rationale: Metformin interferes with B12 absorption increasing deficiency risk after 5 years
what_to_do.implementation: Order serum B12 and methylmalonic acid if borderline
what_to_do.monitoring: Supplement with 1000 mcg cyanocobalamin if deficient

what_to_do.action: Titrate dose slowly over 4 weeks while monitoring gastrointestinal tolerance
what_to_do.rationale: Minimizes discontinuation due to side effects and improves adherence
what_to_do.implementation: Start 500 mg once daily with evening meal and increase weekly
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Greater than 80 percent tolerability at target dose
what_to_do.monitoring: Patient-reported symptom diary at each visit

what_not_to_do.action: Do not initiate or continue metformin if eGFR falls below 30 mL/min/1.73 m2
what_not_to_do.safer_alternative: Switch to DPP-4 inhibitor or SGLT2 inhibitor with renal dosing

what_not_to_do.action: Do not administer iodinated contrast without holding metformin for 48 hours
what_not_to_do.risks: Acute kidney injury precipitating metformin accumulation
what_not_to_do.safer_alternative: Use alternative imaging or confirm eGFR recovery before resumption

what_not_to_do.action: Do not combine with excessive alcohol intake without counseling
what_not_to_do.safer_alternative: Limit to one standard drink per day with blood glucose checks

debunked_claims.claim: Metformin causes significant weight gain in all patients
debunked_claims.reason_debunked: Clinical trials show average weight loss of 1-2 kg or neutrality
debunked_claims.evidence: UKPDS and ADOPT trials demonstrated weight neutrality or loss
debunked_claims.why_harmful: May deter appropriate prescribing leading to poorer glycemic control

debunked_claims.claim: Metformin is completely safe without any renal monitoring
debunked_claims.reason_debunked: FDA black box warning and guidelines mandate periodic eGFR checks
debunked_claims.evidence: Post-marketing surveillance data on lactic acidosis cases
debunked_claims.why_harmful: Delays recognition of accumulating drug levels

debunked_claims.claim: Metformin cures type 2 diabetes and can be stopped after HbA1c normalization
debunked_claims.reason_debunked: It controls but does not cure the progressive disease
debunked_claims.evidence: Long-term studies show need for ongoing therapy or additional agents
debunked_claims.why_harmful: Premature discontinuation results in hyperglycemia rebound

requirements: Serum creatinine and eGFR at baseline then every 3-6 months
Vitamin B12 level annually after 5 years of therapy
Fasting plasma glucose and HbA1c every 3 months until stable
Liver function tests at baseline and as clinically indicated

warning_signs.sign: Unexplained muscle pain, weakness, or fatigue
warning_signs.description: Early indicator of lactic acidosis requiring immediate medical evaluation

warning_signs.sign: Rapid breathing, dizziness, or abdominal pain
warning_signs.description: Suggests metabolic acidosis; discontinue metformin and seek emergency care

warning_signs.sign: Persistent nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea leading to dehydration
warning_signs.description: May precipitate acute kidney injury and drug accumulation
